name: Psychiatric disease therapies module

phase_0.boundary_condition: the blood-brain barrier that closes and contains the neural signaling system

VINIT.justification: the void/uninitialized state before any neurotransmitter is released or any receptor is activated

TANCH.justification: the terminal anchor that contains the entire neural signaling system, preventing external interference

AFWD.justification: a directed forward morphism that carries the signal from soma to synapse

AREV.justification: the reverse/descent transformation that clears neurotransmitters from the synaptic cleft

CLINK.justification: sequential chaining of receptor activation, second messenger systems, and gene expression

IMSCRIB.justification: self-recognition: the neuron senses its own neurotransmitter release and adjusts accordingly

FSPLIT.justification: splits the action potential signal into two branches: neurotransmitter release into the cleft and presynaptic autoreceptor activation

FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes the split signal by summing excitatory and inhibitory postsynaptic potentials at the axon hillock

EVALT.justification: affirmative branch: depolarization that brings the neuron closer to firing threshold

EVALF.justification: negative branch: hyperpolarization that moves the neuron away from firing threshold

ENGAGR.chosen_element: temporal summation of EPSP and IPSP
ENGAGR.justification: a paradice: both excitatory and inhibitory inputs are held simultaneously without immediate resolution, allowing complex integration

IFIX.justification: permanent, irreversible record of synaptic strength change, encoded in receptor density and spine morphology

phase_2.split_input: action potential arriving at the presynaptic terminal
split_outputs: neurotransmitter release into the synaptic cleft
phase_2.fuse_result: summed postsynaptic potential that determines whether the neuron fires

phase_3.void_description: resting membrane potential (~-70 mV) — no neurotransmitter release, no receptor activation, system uninitialized
phase_3.true_description: EPSP — depolarization, increased probability of action potential firing
phase_3.false_description: IPSP — hyperpolarization, decreased probability of action potential firing
phase_3.both_description: temporal summation — simultaneous EPSP and IPSP inputs held at the axon hillock, net effect determined by integration

steps.domain_action: resting membrane potential establishes the baseline void state before any signaling begins
action potential propagates down the axon toward the presynaptic terminal
synaptic vesicle fusion splits the signal into neurotransmitter release and autoreceptor activation
neurotransmitter binds to postsynaptic receptors, generating an EPSP (affirmative branch)
neurotransmitter binds to inhibitory receptors, generating an IPSP (negative branch)
temporal summation holds both EPSP and IPSP simultaneously at the axon hillock without immediate resolution
signal transduction cascade links receptor activation to second messenger systems and gene expression
autoreceptor feedback provides self-recognition, modulating further neurotransmitter release
postsynaptic integration reconstitutes the split signal into a summed postsynaptic potential
reuptake and enzymatic degradation clear neurotransmitters from the synaptic cleft, reversing the signal
long-term potentiation or depression permanently records the synaptic strength change
the blood-brain barrier anchors the entire system, preventing external interference and containing the process

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the presynaptic machinery (SNARE proteins, calcium channels) that translates the action potential into neurotransmitter release
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: neurotransmitter diffusion across the synaptic cleft — the physical communication channel between neurons
phase_5.memory_mechanism: receptor density and spine morphology at the postsynaptic density — stores synaptic strength as a structural state
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the rate of action potential firing and the timing of neurotransmitter release — determines order and timing of synaptic events
phase_5.alfs_store: the genetic blueprint for neurotransmitter synthesis enzymes, receptors, and reuptake transporters — the canonical bootstrap store for neural signaling

phase_6.cycle_cost: ATP consumed by ion pumps (Na+/K+ ATPase) to restore resting membrane potential and by vesicle recycling
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: resting membrane potential maintained by ion gradients
phase_6.post_cycle_state: resting membrane potential restored after signal propagation and neurotransmitter clearance
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is entropy-conservative because ATP hydrolysis drives the restoration of ion gradients, maintaining the system in a steady state far from equilibrium
